stem: A computer has a Central Processing Unit (CPU) that runs programs using the fetch-decode-execute cycle.
model_answer: During the fetch-decode-execute cycle the CPU first fetches an instruction from memory (RAM / primary storage) using registers such as the program counter and MAR/MDR. The instruction is decoded — split into an opcode and operand — by the control unit. Finally the instruction is executed; arithmetic and logical operations are performed by the ALU and the result may be stored in a register before the cycle repeats.
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q6(a). 2 marks. Ignore inaccurate register/component references; credit the 'fetched from memory', 'decoded', 'executed', 'ALU performs arithmetic/logic' ideas.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe what happens during the fetch-execute cycle.

mark_points.text: instructions/data are fetched from memory (RAM / primary storage)
instructions are decoded / split into opcode and operand
instructions are executed / the ALU performs the arithmetic or logic
